headline: Mixed signals dominate as models split on market direction amid geopolitical shifts
summary: Five AI models made 15 predictions for April 1st, 2026, revealing sharp disagreement on market direction despite shared awareness of geopolitical de-escalation and sector-specific catalysts. Three models (Claude, GPT-4o, Grok) heavily favored broad market gains with high confidence in SPY and QQQ rallies, while Perplexity took a contrarian bearish stance on indices. Actual closing prices remain unavailable, leaving all predictions pending final scoring.

consensus_picks.note: Three models consensus on S&P 500 upside despite Perplexity's bearish contrarian call citing geopolitical headwinds.
Tech-focused models bullish on Nasdaq; Perplexity warned of AI investment pullback headwinds.
Claude very confident (78%) in 11% premarket decline; Perplexity neutral citing mixed consumer signals.

best_call.summary: Claude identified nCino's 23% premarket jump on earnings beat with 65% confidence—highest conviction call on a clear catalyst.

worst_call.summary: Grok's bearish TSLA call based on Twitter sentiment had lowest confidence (55%) and vaguest reasoning among directional bets.
